paragraphs: Stella is a sweet cherry bred at the Summerland Research Station in British Columbia, Canada, from a 1956 cross (Lambert with a John Innes seedling) and named in 1968. It is historically important as the first named self-fertile sweet cherry.

The fruit is large, heart-shaped and dark red with good quality, and it ripens about a week before Bing (mid to late season). Because Stella is self-fertile, a single tree will set fruit and it can also pollinate other sweet cherries.

claims.text: Sweet cherry bred at Summerland, British Columbia, from a 1956 cross of Lambert with a John Innes seedling, named 1968
The first named self-fertile sweet cherry
Ripens about a week before Bing (mid to late season)
